text: One of the most important skills for a programmer is writing clean code. Clean code is not just about making the computer understand instructions; it is about making the code easy for humans to read. Using clear and consistent naming conventions is a great start. For example, a variable named `userAge` is much more understandable than `ua`. You should also break your code into small, focused functions that each do one specific thing. This makes the code more organized and easier to test. Ultimately, clean code is essential for long-term maintainability, allowing you or other developers to understand and update the project without confusion.
